Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 396(3).

Section.Text: Section 160 of the Bank Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an officer, director, employee or agent of — or any other person nominated by or acting on behalf of — an eligible agent within the meaning of subsection 370(1);

Section.Text: Subsection 370(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: eligible agent means an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or an agent or agency of a government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whose mandate is publicly available;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that controls the assets of an investment fund in a manner intended to maximize long-term risk-adjusted returns and that fund is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: one to which, as the case may be, Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or the government of a foreign country or political subdivision contributes, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: established to provide compensation, hospitalization, medical care, annuities, pensions or similar benefits to natural persons; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whose decisions with respect to the assets of the fund referred to in paragraph (b) are not influenced in any significant way by, as the case may be, Her Majesty in right of Canada or of the province or the government of the foreign country or the political subdivision.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 371:

Subsection.Text: Despite section 371, for the purpose of determining ownership of a bank by an eligible agent, where two persons, at least one of whom is an eligible agent, are associated with each other, those persons are deemed to be a single eligible agent who beneficially owns the aggregate number of shares of the bank beneficially owned by them.
For the purposes of subsection (1), a person is associated with another person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of the same province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of a government of the same foreign country or a political subdivision of the same foreign country;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one person is Her Majesty in right of Canada and the other person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in that right;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: one person is Her Majesty in right of a province and the other person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of that province; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: one person is a government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country and the other person is its agent or agency.

Section.Text: Section 401.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a bank may, with the Minister’s approval, record in its securities register the issue of any share of the bank to an eligible agent.

The Minister, in determining whether to grant the approval referred to in subsection (3), shall take into account all matters that he or she considers relevant, including those set out in paragraphs 396(1)(a) to (h).

Section.Text: Section 401.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 401.2(3).

Section.Text: Section 750 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an officer, director, employee or agent of — or any other person nominated by or acting on behalf of — an eligible agent within the meaning of subsection 370(1);

Section.Text: Section 873 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 370 to 371.1 apply in respect of bank holding companies, except that references to “bank” in sections 371 and 371.1 are to be read as references to “bank holding company”.
Section 913 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a bank holding company may, with the Minister’s approval, record in its securities register the issue of any share of the bank holding company to an eligible agent.

The Minister, in determining whether to grant the approval referred to in subsection (3), shall take into account all matters that he or she considers relevant, including those set out in paragraphs 906(1)(a) to (g).

Section.Text: Section 914 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 913(3).

Section.Text: Subsection 168(1) of the Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an officer, director, employee or agent of — or any other person nominated by or acting on behalf of — an eligible agent within the meaning of section 406.1;

Section.Text: Section 406.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: agent means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province, any agent of Her Majesty in either of those rights, and includes a municipal or public body empowered to perform a function of government in Canada or any entity empowered to perform a function or duty on behalf of Her Majesty in either of those rights but does not include

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an official or entity performing a function or duty in connection with the administration or management of the estate or property of a natural person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an official or entity performing a function or duty in connection with the administration, management or investment of a fund established to provide compensation, hospitalization, medical care, annuities, pensions or similar benefits to natural persons, or moneys derived from such a fund, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trustee of any trust for the administration of a fund to which Her Majesty in either of those rights contributes and of which an official or entity that is an agent of Her Majesty in either of those rights is a trustee; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision thereof, a person empowered to perform a function or duty on behalf of the government of the foreign country or political subdivision, other than a function or duty in connection with the administration or management of the estate or property of a natural person.

Definition.Text: eligible agent means an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or an agent or agency of a government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whose mandate is publicly available;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that controls the assets of an investment fund in a manner intended to maximize long-term risk-adjusted returns and that fund is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: one to which, as the case may be, Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or the government of a foreign country or political subdivision contributes, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: established to provide compensation, hospitalization, medical care, annuities, pensions or similar benefits to natural persons; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whose decisions with respect to the assets of the fund referred to in paragraph (b) are not influenced in any significant way by, as the case may be, Her Majesty in right of Canada or of the province or the government of the foreign country or the political subdivision.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of determining ownership of a company by an eligible agent, where two persons, at least one of whom is an eligible agent, are associated with each other, those persons are deemed to be a single eligible agent who beneficially owns the aggregate number of shares of the company beneficially owned by them.
For the purposes of subsection (1), a person is associated with another person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of the same province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each person is an agent or agency of a government of the same foreign country or a political subdivision of the same foreign country;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one person is Her Majesty in right of Canada and the other person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in that right;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: one person is Her Majesty in right of a province and the other person is an agent or agency of Her Majesty in right of that province; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: one person is a government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country and the other person is its agent or agency.

Section.Text: Section 428 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a company may, with the Minister’s approval, record in its securities register the issue of any share of the company to an eligible agent.

The Minister, in determining whether to grant the approval referred to in subsection (3), shall take into account all matters that he or she considers relevant, including those set out in paragraphs 420(1)(a) to (h).

Section.Text: Section 430 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 428(3).

Section.Text: Subsection 432(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, with respect to any company, a person contravenes subsection 407(1), (4), (9), (11) or (15) or section 407.03, 407.1 or 407.2 or fails to comply with an undertaking referred to in subsection 416(2) or with any term or condition imposed under section 421, the Minister may, if the Minister considers it in the public interest to do so, by order, direct that person and any person controlled by that person to dispose of any number of shares of the company beneficially owned by any of those persons that the Minister specifies in the order, within the time specified in the order and in the proportion, if any, as between the person and the persons controlled by that person that is specified in the order.

Section.Text: Section 797 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an officer, director, employee or agent of — or any other person nominated by or acting on behalf of — an eligible agent within the meaning of section 406.1;

Section.Text: Section 926 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 406.1 and 406.2 apply in respect of insurance holding companies, except that references to “company” in that section 406.2 are to be read as references to “insurance holding company”.
Section 954 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), an insurance holding company may, with the Minister’s approval, record in its securities register the issue of any share of the insurance holding company to an eligible agent.

The Minister, in determining whether to grant the approval referred to in subsection (3), shall take into account all matters that he or she considers relevant, including those set out in paragraphs 947(1)(a) to (g).

Section.Text: Section 955 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 955(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 954(3).
In this section, other Act means the Jobs and Economic Growth Act.

On the first day on which both subsection 401.3(3) of the Bank Act, as enacted by section 2072 of the other Act, and subsection 401.3(3) of the Bank Act, as enacted by section 334 of this Act, are in force, subsection 401.3(3) of the Bank Act, as enacted by section 334 of this Act, is renumbered as subsection 401.3(4) and is repositioned accordingly if required.
In this section, other Act means the Financial System Review Act.
If section 157 of the other Act comes into force before section 347 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 347 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 955 of the Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of the exercise of voting rights attached to any share referred to in subsection 954(3).
If section 347 of this Act comes into force before section 157 of the other Act, then that section 157 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Section 955 is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a foreign institution that is controlled by the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country, or by any agent or agency of a foreign government, if the shares referred to in subsection (1) are beneficially owned by the foreign institution or by an entity controlled by the foreign institution.

If section 157 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 347 of this Act, then that section 157 is deemed to have come into force before that section 347 and subsection (2) applies as a consequence.

Section.Text: The definition regulation in section 2 of the National Housing Act is repealed.
Section 7 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: président de la Société d’assurance-dépôts du Canada

Definition.Text: Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation means the Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation appointed under section 6 of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: commissaire de l’Agence de la consommation en matière financière du Canada

Definition.Text: Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada means the Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada appointed under section 4 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: Governor of the Bank of Canada

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: gouverneur de la Banque du Canada

Definition.Text: Governor of the Bank of Canada means the Governor of the Bank of Canada appointed under section 6 of the Bank of Canada Act.
housing loan means a loan that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relates to a housing project;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is secured at least in part by security on or affecting a housing project or by an agreement relating to the use, occupancy or disposal of a housing project;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is made for the purpose of acquiring a financial interest in a person who, directly or indirectly, owns, leases or operates a housing project; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is made for the purpose of refinancing a debt that the Corporation is of the opinion relates to a housing project or to a financial interest referred to in paragraph (c).

Definition.Text: Superintendent means the Superintendent of Financial Institutions appointed under section 5 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act.

Section.Text: The Corporation has the following objects in carrying on any activities under this Part or Part I.1:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to promote the efficient functioning and competitiveness of the housing finance market;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to promote and contribute to the stability of the financial system, including the housing market; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to have due regard to the Corporation’s exposure to loss.

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Corporation may — with the Minister of Finance’s approval and on any terms or conditions that are specified by him or her — guarantee payment of any or all of the principal or interest, or both, in respect of securities issued on the basis of housing loans. The terms and conditions may, among others, relate to the amount and price of the guarantee, the characteristics of the securities to be guaranteed and the manner in which the securities are to be issued.
The Governor in Council may, on the Minister of Finance’s recommendation, make regulations respecting the characteristics of those who may issue or sell the securities.
The Minister of Finance may make regulations respecting the characteristics of the housing loans.

The Minister of Finance may fix a fee to be paid by the Corporation to the Receiver General to compensate Her Majesty for Her exposure to the risks arising from a guarantee referred to in subsection (1). That Minister shall notify the Corporation in writing of the fee.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 14:
It is prohibited for the Corporation to guarantee payment of any or all of the principal or interest, or both, in respect of a covered bond as defined in section 21.5.
Section 15 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite anything in this Act other than subsection (2), the total of the outstanding guaranteed amounts of principal for all issues of securities in respect of which guarantees that have been given under section 14 are in force may not exceed the total amount provided for in section 11.
The total of the outstanding guaranteed amounts of principal may not exceed the amount authorized by Parliament under an appropriation Act or other Act of Parliament.

Section.Text: The portion of section 19 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to any regulations made under this Part, the Corporation may
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 21.4:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: affiliate means an affiliate as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act.

covered bond means, except in paragraph 21.53(b), a debt obligation in relation to which the principal and interest owing are guaranteed to be paid from the loans or other assets held by a guarantor entity.

covered bond collateral means the loans or other assets that secure the payment of principal, interest and any other amounts owing in relation to the covered bonds that are issued under a registered program.
derivatives agreement means a financial agreement whose obligations are derived from, referenced to, or based on, one or more underlying reference items.

guarantor entity means an entity that is created and organized for the principal purpose of holding loans or other assets as the covered bond collateral for a registered program with the intention of legally isolating those loans or other assets from the registered issuer.
registered issuer means an institution that is registered in the registry under section 21.53.
registered program means a program that is registered in the registry under section 21.55.
registry means the registry established under section 21.51.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation must establish and maintain a registry containing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the names and business addresses of registered issuers;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a list of registered programs and information relating to those programs, including the name of each essential service provider to the guarantor entity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a list of registered issuers whose right to issue covered bonds is suspended under subsection 21.62(1) and the reasons for the suspension;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that, in the Corporation’s opinion, is necessary; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation must make the registry accessible to the public through the Internet and by any other means that it considers appropriate.
Any of the following institutions may apply for registration as a registered issuer:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a federal financial institution as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a cooperative credit society that is incorporated and regulated by or under an Act of the legislature of a province.

Subsection.Text: The applicant must provide the Corporation, in the time, manner and form required by the Corporation, with the required fees and any information that, in the Corporation’s opinion, is required for the purposes of the registration.

Section.Text: The Corporation may register an institution in the registry if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the Corporation’s opinion, all of the requirements under this Part have been met; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the institution gives an undertaking to not issue any debt obligation that is commonly known as a covered bond except under a registered program.

Subsection.Text: A registered issuer may apply for registration of a program under which covered bonds are to be issued.
The registered issuer must provide the Corporation, in the time, manner and form required by the Corporation, with the required fees and the information referred to in subsection (3).
An application must contain a complete description of the program, including the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the entity that will act as the guarantor entity and the type of entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the loans or other assets to be held as the covered bond collateral and their value;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the minimum and maximum ratio required under the program of the value of the covered bonds to be issued to the value of the loans or other assets to be held as covered bond collateral;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the contractual relationships between the registered issuer and the entity that will act as the guarantor entity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the names of all essential service providers to the entity that will act as the guarantor entity, a description of the services to be provided and, if a registered issuer or one of its affiliates is acting as a service provider, the circumstances in which it must be replaced;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the names of any parties to a derivatives agreement with the entity that will act as the guarantor entity; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that, in the Corporation’s opinion, is required.

Section.Text: The Corporation may register the program in the registry if, in the Corporation’s opinion, all of the requirements under this Part have been met.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation must notify the applicant in writing of its decision with respect to the application to register the applicant or the program.
An applicant may withdraw the application for registration by so notifying the Corporation in writing at any time before the day on which the applicant receives notice of the Corporation’s decision.
The Corporation may deregister a registered program on the request of the registered issuer only if there are no covered bonds outstanding under the program.
The Corporation may deregister a registered issuer on the request of the issuer only if the issuer has no registered programs.

Section.Text: The Corporation may, at any time, establish conditions or restrictions applicable to registered issuers and registered programs.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation may establish fees for registration applications under subsection 21.52(1) and subsection 21.54(1) as well as other fees to be paid by registered issuers.

The fees must be established, in accordance with any regulations, to be commensurate with the costs incurred by the Corporation in exercising its powers and performing its duties and functions under this Part.
Only the following assets may be held as covered bond collateral:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: loans made on the security of residential property that is located in Canada and consists of not more than four residential units; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed assets.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), covered bond collateral may include securities that are issued by the Government of Canada and any prescribed assets.

Unless regulations have been made under paragraph 21.66(g), the value of the assets described in subsection (2) must not exceed 10 per cent of the total value of the loans or other assets held as covered bond collateral.
Despite subsections (1) and (2), the following loans must not be held as covered bond collateral:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the loan is insured by the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the loan is insured by the Canada Guaranty Mortgage Insurance Company, the Genworth Financial Mortgage Insurance Company Canada, the PMI Mortgage Insurance Company Canada or any successor to any of those companies; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage or hypothecary loan having an equal or prior claim against the property, exceeds 80 per cent of the value of the property at the time of the loan.

Subsection.Text: Subject to any other provision of this Part or Part I, any information that is collected by the Corporation under this Part is confidential and must be treated accordingly.
Information that is collected by the Corporation under this Part must be used by the Corporation only for the purpose for which it is collected.
The Corporation may suspend the right of a registered issuer to issue further covered bonds under a registered program.

In the case where the Corporation decides to suspend that right, it must provide the registered issuer with a written notice of and the reasons for the intended suspension, no later than 30 days before the day on which the suspension is to take effect.
The Corporation must provide a copy of the notice and reasons to,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a federal financial institution as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, the Superintendent of Financial Institutions appointed under subsection 5(1) of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a cooperative credit society incorporated and regulated by or under an Act of the legislature of a province, the body that regulates that society.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation may revoke a suspension and, in that case, must provide the registered issuer with written notice of the revocation of the suspension.

Section.Text: Nothing in any law of Canada or a province relating to bankruptcy or insolvency, or any order of a court made in relation to a reorganization, arrangement or receivership involving bankruptcy or insolvency, prevents or prohibits the following actions from being taken in accordance with the provisions of contracts relating to covered bonds that are issued under a registered program:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the making of any payments, including a payment to a registered issuer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the netting or setting off or compensation of obligations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any dealing with covered bond collateral, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the sale or foreclosure or, in Quebec, the surrender of covered bond collateral, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the setting off or compensation of covered bond collateral or the application of the proceeds or value of covered bond collateral; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the termination of those contracts.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer not voidable and not subject to remedies
Section.Text: Despite anything in any law of Canada or a province relating to bankruptcy or insolvency, or any order of a court made in relation to a reorganization, arrangement or receivership involving bankruptcy or insolvency, the transfer of loans or other assets to a guarantor entity — to be held as covered bond collateral — by a registered issuer, any of its affiliates or any prescribed entity

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is effective against every person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not voidable or, in Quebec, annullable;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is not subject to any other remedies available to creditors of the registered issuer; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: does not constitute a fraudulent conveyance, unjust preference or other reviewable transaction.

Section.Text: Sections 21.63 and 21.64 do not apply to contracts relating to covered bonds that are issued under a registered program, nor to the transfer of loans or other assets to a guarantor entity to be held as covered bond collateral for those covered bonds, if those covered bonds are issued by a registered issuer during the period in which its right to issue covered bonds has been suspended under section 21.62.
The Minister of Finance may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Part, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: excluding, from the definition covered bond, certain debt obligations;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: excluding, from the definition covered bond collateral, certain payments from the guarantor entity to the registered issuer;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establishing requirements that an institution, or a program under which covered bonds are to be issued, must meet to be registered in the registry;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting applications made under section 21.52 or 21.54;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting cost recovery for the purposes of section 21.59;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: establishing additional requirements with respect to the loans referred to in paragraph 21.6(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which guarantor entities may hold the assets described in subsection 21.6(2) and, according to each circumstance, the maximum ratio of the value of those assets relative to the total value of the loans or other assets being held as covered bond collateral; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that is to be prescribed under this Part.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Supporting Vulnerable Seniors and Strengthening Canada’s Economy Act

Section.Text: Section 23 of the Supporting Vulnerable Seniors and Strengthening Canada’s Economy Act is amended by replacing the subsection 8.1(1) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance may, after consulting the Governor of the Bank of Canada and the Superintendent, make regulations respecting classes of housing loans and the criteria to be met by loans in each of those classes in order for the Corporation to be able to provide insurance against risks relating to those loans.

Section.Text: Section 24 of the Act is amended by replacing the section 21.1 that it enacts with the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Obligation to retain information, books and records

Subsection.Text: The Corporation shall keep and retain books and records, and retain information, in respect of its activities under this Part and Part I.1, including any books, records or information that are prescribed by regulation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obligation to provide information or copies
Subsection.Text: At the request of the Minister of Finance, the Corporation shall, without delay, provide that Minister with any information or copies of any books or records that it is required to retain.

The Minister of Finance may disclose to the Superintendent, the Governor of the Bank of Canada, the Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation and the Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada any information or copies of any books or records received under subsection (2).
The Corporation shall make available to the public the books, records and information that are prescribed by regulation.

The Governor in Council may, on the Minister of Finance’s recommendation, make regulations respecting the manner in which books and records are to be kept and retained, the manner in which information is to be retained and the manner in which books, records and information are to be made available to the public.

The Superintendent, from time to time but at least once in each calendar year, shall make or cause to be made any examination or inquiry that the Superintendent considers to be necessary or expedient to determine whether the Corporation is carrying on any or all of its activities under this Part and Part I.1 in a safe and sound manner, including whether it is carrying on those activities with due regard to its exposure to loss.
For the purposes of the examination or inquiry, the Superintendent or a person acting under his or her direction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a right of access to any books or records that are held by or on behalf of the Corporation in respect of its activities; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may require the Corporation’s directors, officers and auditors to provide information and explanations to the Superintendent, to the extent that they are reasonably able to do so, in respect of the Corporation’s activities.

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent shall report the results of the examination or inquiry, including any recommendations, to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation’s board of directors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister and the Minister of Finance.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation’s corporate plan that is required under section 122 of the Financial Administration Act must contain a proposal indicating how the Corporation will address the recommendations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obligation to provide information or copies
Subsection.Text: At the request of the Superintendent, the Corporation shall, without delay, provide him or her with any information or copies of any books or records that it is required to retain in respect of its activities under this Part and Part I.1.

Subject to any other provision of this Act, all information in respect of the Corporation’s activities under this Part and Part I.1, including regarding a person dealing with the Corporation, that is obtained by the Superintendent or a person acting under his or her direction is confidential and shall be treated accordingly.
The Superintendent may disclose any information or copies of any books or records that are received under this Part or Part I.1 to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister and the Minister of Finance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Governor of the Bank of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Chairperson of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Commissioner of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent shall, before December 31 in each year, ascertain the total amount of expenses incurred during the preceding fiscal year in connection with the administration of section 21.2.
The Corporation shall pay the amount within 30 days after the day on which the Superintendent notifies it in writing of the amount.
For the purpose of subsection (1), the fiscal year is the period beginning on April 1 in one year and ending on March 31 in the next year.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the Minister of Finance’s recommendation, make regulations respecting the Corporation’s activities under this Part, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conditions and limitations to which those activities are subject;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the terms and conditions on which and manner in which the Corporation may exercise its powers under this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that is to be prescribed by this Part.

Section.Text: Despite section 26 of the Supporting Vulnerable Seniors and Strengthening Canada’s Economy Act, sections 22 to 24 of that Act come into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act

Section.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Board of Directors shall consist of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Chairperson;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the President;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister’s Deputy Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Deputy Minister of Finance; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: eight other directors.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (4) to (6) do not apply to the directors referred to in paragraphs (1)(c) and (d).
A director referred to in paragraph (1)(c) or (d) may designate an alternate to attend in the director’s absence at any meeting of the Board of Directors.

Section.Text: The Trust and Loan Companies Act is amended by adding the following after section 415:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for a company to issue a debt obligation in relation to which the amounts of principal and interest owing are guaranteed to be paid from loans or other assets held by an entity that is created and organized for the principal purpose of holding those loans or other assets and with the intention of legally isolating those loans or other assets from the company, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is a covered bond as defined in section 21.5 of the National Housing Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company is a registered issuer as defined in section 21.5 of that Act other than one whose right to issue covered bonds has been suspended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is issued under a registered program as defined in section 21.5 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting any type of debt obligation from the application of subsection (1).

Section.Text: The Bank Act is amended by adding the following after section 415:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for a bank to issue a debt obligation in relation to which the amounts of principal and interest owing are guaranteed to be paid from loans or other assets held by an entity that is created and organized for the principal purpose of holding those loans or other assets and with the intention of legally isolating those loans or other assets from the bank, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is a covered bond as defined in section 21.5 of the National Housing Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the bank is a registered issuer as defined in section 21.5 of that Act other than one whose right to issue covered bonds has been suspended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is issued under a registered program as defined in section 21.5 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting any type of debt obligation from the application of subsection (1).

Section.Text: The Insurance Companies Act is amended by adding the following after section 468:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for a company to issue a debt obligation in relation to which the amounts of principal and interest owing are guaranteed to be paid from loans or other assets held by an entity that is created and organized for the principal purpose of holding those loans or other assets and with the intention of legally isolating those loans or other assets from the company, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is a covered bond as defined in section 21.5 of the National Housing Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the company is a registered issuer as defined in section 21.5 of that Act other than one whose right to issue covered bonds has been suspended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is issued under a registered program as defined in section 21.5 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting any type of debt obligation from the application of subsection (1).

Section.Text: The Cooperative Credit Associations Act is amended by adding the following after section 380:

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for an association to issue a debt obligation in relation to which the amounts of principal and interest owing are guaranteed to be paid from loans or other assets held by an entity that is created and organized for the principal purpose of holding those loans or other assets and with the intention of legally isolating those loans or other assets from the association, unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is a covered bond as defined in section 21.5 of the National Housing Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the association is a registered issuer as defined in section 21.5 of that Act other than one whose right to issue covered bonds has been suspended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the debt obligation is issued under a registered program as defined in section 21.5 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting any type of debt obligation from the application of subsection (1).

Section.MarginalNote: Guarantees made before section 352 comes into force
Section.Text: A guarantee made by the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation under section 14 of the National Housing Act before the day on which section 352 of this Act comes into force does not require the Minister’s approval.

On the first day on which both Part 7 of the Supporting Vulnerable Seniors and Strengthening Canada’s Economy Act and subsection 21.6(4) of the National Housing Act, as enacted by section 356, are in force, the National Housing Act is amended by replacing that subsection 21.6(4) with the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) and (2), the following loans must not be held as covered bond collateral:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the loan is insured by the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the loan is insured and that insurance is protected under the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made on the security of residential property if the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage or hypothecary loan having an equal or prior claim against the property, exceeds 80 per cent of the value of the property at the time of the loan.

Subsection.Text: Sections 352 and 354 come into force on the later of January 1, 2013 and the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Paragraph 21.52(1)(b) of the National Housing Act, as enacted by section 356, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 361 to 364 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act

Section.Text: The Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to implement a Framework Agreement on Integrated Cross-Border Law Enforment Operations between the Government of Canada and the Government of the United States of America

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agreement means the Framework Agreement on Integrated Cross-Border Maritime Law Enforcement Operations between the Government of Canada and the Government of the United States of America signed on May 26, 2009.
designated officer means an individual appointed as a cross-border maritime law enforcement officer by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Central Authority for Canada under subsection 7(1) or 8(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person designated as the Central Authority for the United States for the application of the Agreement.

Definition.Text: integrated cross-border operation means the deployment of a vessel crewed jointly by designated officers from Canada and the United States for cross-border law enforcement purposes in undisputed areas of the sea or internal waters along the international boundary between Canada and the United States.

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is to implement the Agreement, the objectives of which are to provide additional means to prevent, detect and suppress criminal offences and violations of the law in undisputed areas of the sea or internal waters along the international boundary between Canada and the United States and to facilitate the investigation and prosecution of such offences and violations.
It is recognized and declared that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Canada and the United States have a common interest in the security of the undisputed areas of the sea or internal waters along the international boundary between Canada and the United States;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: integrated cross-border operations must

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: respect the sovereignty of Canada and the United States,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: be conducted in accordance with the rule of law, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: be conducted as directed by a designated officer from the host country; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in Canada, integrated cross-border operations must be conducted in a manner respecting the rights and freedoms guaranteed by the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

Section.Text: For the purposes of implementing the Agreement, the Central Authority for Canada is the Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police or his or her delegate.

The Central Authority for Canada has, in cooperation with the person designated as the Central Authority for the United States for the purposes of implementing the Agreement, responsibility for the direction and management of integrated cross-border operations.

Subsection.Text: The Central Authority for Canada may appoint as a cross-border maritime law enforcement officer an individual who is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a member of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a police officer appointed or employed under the law of a province; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a pilot, co-pilot, observer or other member of the crew of an aircraft that is operated by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police or a police service established under the law of a province and that is used to provide aerial support in an integrated cross-border operation.

Subsection.Text: An individual may be appointed under subsection (1) only if they have satisfactorily completed the required training, approved by the Central Authority for Canada, for appointment as a designated officer.

Section.MarginalNote: Appointment of officers from United States

Subsection.Text: The Central Authority for Canada may appoint as a cross-border maritime law enforcement officer an individual who is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a commissioned, warrant or petty officer of the United States Coast Guard;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a police officer or other law enforcement officer appointed or employed under the law of the United States or of a state of the United States; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a pilot, co-pilot, observer or other member of the crew of an aircraft that is operated by the United States Coast Guard or a police service, or other law enforcement agency of the United States or of a state of the United States, and that is used to provide aerial support in an integrated cross-border operation.

Subsection.Text: An individual may be appointed under subsection (1) only if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they have been recommended for appointment by the person designated as the Central Authority for the United States for the application of the Agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: they have satisfactorily completed the required training, approved by the Central Authority for Canada, for appointment as a designated officer.

Section.Text: The Central Authority for Canada may suspend or revoke the appointment of any designated officer.

Subsection.Text: The Central Authority for Canada may issue a certificate to any designated officer stating that the person to whom it is issued is a designated officer for the purposes of this Act.
Any document purporting to be a certificate referred to in subsection (1) is evidence in all courts and in all proceedings of the facts stated in it.

Section.Text: The Central Authority for Canada may recommend, to the person designated as the Central Authority for the United States for the purposes of implementing the Agreement, that an individual described in paragraph 7(1)(a), (b) or (c) be appointed as a cross-border maritime law enforcement officer in the United States.
Every designated officer has the same power to enforce an Act of Parliament as a member of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police when

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: participating in an integrated cross-border operation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in an activity incidental to such an operation, including travel for the purpose of participating in the operation and appearances in court arising from the operation.

Subsection.Text: The laws of Canada apply to any person detained or taken into custody within Canada in the course of an integrated cross-border operation.
No person referred to in subsection (1) may be removed from Canada, except in accordance with the laws of Canada.
The laws of Canada apply to any vessel or other thing seized within Canada in the course of an integrated cross-border operation.
Subject to subsection (3), no vessel or other thing referred to in subsection (1) may be removed from Canada, except in accordance with the laws of Canada.
A vessel or other thing referred to in subsection (1) may be removed from Canada in situations of operational or geographical necessity, including situations in which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before delivering the vessel or thing to the place where it is to be delivered in Canada, designated officers are required to participate in a continuing integrated cross-border operation or respond to an emergency in the waters of the United States;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: due to poor weather or mechanical difficulties with a vessel operated by designated officers, it is necessary to transit through the waters of the United States in order to reach the nearest port; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the navigable shipping channels between the location in Canada where the vessel or thing was seized and the place where the vessel or thing is to be delivered in Canada pass through the waters of the United States.

Section.MarginalNote: Vessel, etc., seized in United States
Section.Text: A vessel or other thing seized in the United States in the course of an integrated cross-border operation remains in the custody and control of the American designated officer if it is brought into Canada in situations of operational or geographical necessity, including situations in which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before delivering the vessel or thing to the place where it is to be delivered in the United States, designated officers are required to participate in a continuing integrated cross-border operation or respond to an emergency in the waters of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: due to poor weather or mechanical difficulties with a vessel operated by designated officers, it is necessary to transit through the waters of Canada in order to reach the nearest port; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the navigable shipping channels between the location in the United States where the vessel or thing was seized and the place where the vessel or thing is to be delivered in the United States pass through the waters of Canada.

Section.Text: No Act of Parliament relating to the import or export of goods applies to the import or export of a vessel or other thing by a designated officer in the circumstances described in subsection 14(3) or section 15.

Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act

Section.Text: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act is amended by adding the following after section 45.47:

Heading.TitleText: Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in sections 45.49 to 45.51.

Definition.Text: Central Authority means the Central Authority for Canada, as designated under section 5 of the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act.
designated officer has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act.
integrated cross-border operation has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act.

Subsection.Text: Any member of the public who has a complaint concerning the conduct of a designated officer in the performance of any duty or function in the course of an integrated cross-border operation may, whether or not that member of the public is affected by the subject matter of the complaint, make a complaint to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Commission;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any member, as defined in subsection 2(1) or other person appointed or employed under the authority of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the provincial authority in the province in which the subject matter of the complaint arose that is responsible for the receipt and investigation of complaints by the public against police.

Subsection.Text: Every complaint under subsection (1) shall be acknowledged in writing.
The Central Authority shall be notified of every complaint made under subsection (1), and the Commission shall be notified of every complaint made under paragraph (1)(b) or (c).

On being notified of the complaint, the Central Authority shall notify, in writing, the designated officer whose conduct is the subject matter of the complaint of its substance unless, in the Central Authority’s opinion, to do so might adversely affect or hinder any investigation that is being or may be carried out in respect of the complaint.
Sections 45.36 to 45.47 apply in respect of a complaint made under subsection 45.49(1), with the following modifications:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to the Commissioner is a reference to the Central Authority;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to a member or other person whose conduct is the subject matter of the complaint is a reference to a designated officer whose conduct is the subject matter of the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to subsection 45.35(1) is a reference to subsection 45.49(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a reference, other than in paragraph 45.41(2)(b), to the Force is a reference to the person or persons designated for that purpose by the Central Authority; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the reference in paragraph 45.41(2)(b) to the Force is a reference to the Central Authority.

Subsection.Text: An investigation under paragraph 45.42(3)(c) may be carried out jointly with a body designated by the Commission Chairman.

Reports referred to in section 45.4 and subsection 45.46(3) shall also be sent to the minister responsible for policing in the province where the conduct that is the subject matter of the complaint took place.

Section.Text: The Commission Chairman shall send the report referred to in section 45.34 to the minister responsible for policing in each province where integrated cross-border operations took place during that year.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 50:
Paragraph 50(a) does not apply to a designated officer as defined in section 45.48 who was appointed under subsection 8(1) of the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act.
The definition peace officer in section 2 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: a designated officer as defined in section 2 of the Integrated Cross-border Law Enforcement Operations Act, when

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: participating in an integrated cross-border operation, as defined in section 2 of that Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: engaging in an activity incidental to such an operation, including travel for the purpose of participating in the operation and appearances in court arising from the operation;

Section.Text: Paragraphs 11(6)(a) and (b) of the Customs Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: holds an authorization issued by the Minister under subsection 11.1(1) to present himself or herself in a prescribed alternative manner and who presents himself or herself in the manner authorized for that person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is a member of a prescribed class of persons authorized by regulations made under subsection 11.1(3) to present himself or herself in a prescribed alternative manner and who presents himself or herself in the manner authorized for that class.

Section.Text: Paragraph 11.1(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing classes of persons who are, and classes of persons who may be, authorized to present themselves in alternative manners;

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister of Finance may provide for payment out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the International Monetary Fund in the manner and at the times provided for by the Agreement set out in Schedule I of a sum or sums of money, not exceeding in the whole an amount equivalent to the subscriptions required from or permitted to be made by Canada, namely, eleven billion, twenty-three million, nine hundred thousand Special Drawing Rights.
Section 13 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister of Finance shall cause to be laid before Parliament, on or before September 30 next following the end of each calendar year or, if Parliament is not then sitting, on any of the first thirty days next thereafter that either House of Parliament is sitting, a report containing a general summary of operations under this Act and details of all those operations that directly affect Canada, including the resources and lending of the World Bank Group, the funds subscribed or contributed by Canada, borrowings in Canada and procurement of Canadian goods and services.
Paragraph (b) of the definition insured person in section 2 of the Canada Health Act is repealed.
The definition Inspector General in section 2 of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act is repealed.
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: The Director shall, in relation to every 12-month period or any lesser period that is specified by the Minister, submit to the Minister, at any times that the Minister specifies, reports with respect to the Service’s operational activities during that period, and shall cause the Review Committee to be given a copy of each such report.

Section.Text: The heading before section 30 and sections 30 to 33 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 38(a)(i) of the Act is repealed.
Section 38 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 38(1) and is amended by adding the following:

As soon as the circumstances permit after receiving a copy of a report referred to in subsection 6(4), the Review Committee shall submit to the Minister a certificate stating the extent to which it is satisfied with the report and whether any of the Service’s operational activities described in the report, in its opinion,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not authorized by or under this Act or contravenes any directions issued by the Minister under subsection 6(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: involves an unreasonable or unnecessary exercise by the Service of any of its powers.

Section.Text: Paragraph 39(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to have access to any information under the control of the Service that relates to the performance of the duties and functions of the Committee and to receive from the Director and employees such information, reports and explanations as the Committee deems necessary for the performance of its duties and functions; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 40(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: direct the Service to conduct a review of the Service’s specific activities and provide the Committee with a report on the review; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if it considers that a review by the Service would be inappropriate, conduct such a review itself.

Subsection.Text: Section 40 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 40(1) and is amended by adding the following:
On completion of a review conducted under subsection (1), the Review Committee shall provide the Minister and the Director with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a review conducted by the Service, the Service’s report to the Committee along with any recommendations that the Committee considers appropriate; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a review conducted by the Committee, its own report, which is to contain the findings of the review and any recommendations that the Committee considers appropriate.

Section.Text: Section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: At least once a year, and at any other time at the Minister’s request, the Review Committee, or a person engaged by it and designated by it for the purposes of this section, shall meet the Minister and brief him or her on any matter that relates to the performance by the Service of its duties and functions.

The Review Committee may, on request by the Minister or at any other time, furnish the Minister with a special report concerning any matter that relates to the performance of its duties and functions.

Section.Text: Schedule I to the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out the following under the heading “OTHER GOVERNMENT INSTITUTIONS”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Office of the Inspector General of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau de l’Inspecteur général du service canadien du renseignement de sécurité

Section.Text: The schedule to the Security of Information Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Office of the Inspector General of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau de l’Inspecteur général du Service canadien du renseignement de sécurité

Section.Text: The schedule to the Privacy Act is amended by striking out the following under the heading “OTHER GOVERNMENT INSTITUTIONS”:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Office of the Inspector General of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Bureau de l’Inspecteur général du service canadien du renseignement de sécurité

Section.Text: Subsection 8(4) of the Currency Act is repealed.
Section 9 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, call in coins of any date and denomination.
A coin that has been called in is not current.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for the redemption by the Minister of coins of the currency of Canada that are or that have at any time been current in Canada.
Payments for the redemption of coins, including related costs, shall be made out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund on the authorization of the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 3.12 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Additional fiscal equalization payment — 2012-2013 fiscal year
Subsection.Text: An additional fiscal equalization payment may be paid for the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2012 equal to,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for Nova Scotia, $13,471,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for New Brunswick, $102,767,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for Manitoba, $201,295,000.

Section.Text: Part V of the Act is repealed.

The heading “CANADA HEALTH TRANSFER, CANADA SOCIAL TRANSFER, HEALTH REFORM TRANSFER, WAIT TIMES REDUCTION TRANSFER AND EARLY LEARNING AND CHILD CARE TRANSFER” of Part V.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: CANADA HEALTH TRANSFER, CANADA SOCIAL TRANSFER AND WAIT TIMES REDUCTION TRANSFER

Section.Text: Paragraph 24.1(1)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by replacing subparagraph (iv) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the product obtained by multiplying the cash contribution for the immediately preceding year by 1.06, rounded to the nearest thousand, for each fiscal year in the period beginning on April 1, 2006 and ending on March 31, 2017, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2017, the product, rounded to the nearest thousand, obtained by multiplying the cash contribution for the immediately preceding fiscal year by the greater of 1.03, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the average of the annual rates of growth of the nominal gross domestic product of Canada, as determined by the Minister, for the calendar year that ends during the fiscal year in question and for the two previous calendar years; and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 24.2(1) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The cash contribution established under paragraph 24.1(1)(a) that may be provided to a province for each fiscal year in the period beginning on April 1, 2004 and ending on March 31, 2014 is the amount determined by the formula

Section.Text: The portion of section 24.21 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Provincial share — fiscal year 2014-2015 and later
Section.Text: The cash contribution established under paragraph 24.1(1)(a) that may be provided to a province for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2014 is the amount determined by multiplying the total of the cash contributions to be provided to all the provinces for that fiscal year by the quotient obtained by dividing
Paragraph 24.3(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: maintaining the national standard, set out in section 25.1, that no period of minimum residency be required or allowed with respect to social assistance; and

Section.Text: Subparagraph 24.4(1)(a)(vii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the product obtained by multiplying the cash contribution for the immediately preceding fiscal year by 1.03, rounded to the nearest thousand, for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2009.

Section.Text: Section 24.6 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 24.701 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prevention of transfer declines — fiscal year 2014-2015 and later
Subsection.Text: The Minister may pay to a province an additional cash payment for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2014 equal to the amount by which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the cash contribution established under paragraph 24.1(1)(a) to be provided to that province for the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2013, as determined by the Minister between September 1, 2013 and October 12, 2013,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the cash contribution established under paragraph 24.1(1)(a) to be provided to that province for each of those fiscal years as calculated under this Act as it reads on the day on which this subsection comes into force.

Section.Text: Section 24.71 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
The portion of section 25 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Reduction or withholding — Canada Health Transfer and Canada Social Transfer
Section.Text: The cash contribution that may be provided to a province under section 24.2, 24.21, 24.5 or 24.51 is to be reduced or withheld for the purposes of giving effect to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any order made by the Governor in Council in respect of the province under section 15 or 16 of the Canada Health Act or, in the case of a cash contribution under section 24.5 or 24.51, section 25.3 or 25.4 of this Act; or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 25.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Criteria for eligibility — Canada Social Transfer
Section.Text: In order that a province may qualify for a full cash contribution under sections 24.5 and 24.51 for a fiscal year, the laws of the province must not

Subsection.Text: Subsection 25.1(2) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 25.3(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: direct that any cash contribution under section 24.5 or 24.51 to that province for a fiscal year be reduced, in respect of each non-compliance, by an amount that the Governor in Council considers to be appropriate, having regard to the gravity of the non-compliance; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the Governor in Council considers it appropriate, direct that the whole of any cash contribution under section 24.5 or 24.51 to that province for a fiscal year be withheld.

Section.Text: Section 25.7 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every reference to “Canada Health and Social Transfer” in any other Act of Parliament is to be read as a reference to “Canada Health Transfer and the Canada Social Transfer”.
Sections 28 to 30 of the Act are replaced by the following:

The amount of additional tax abatement applicable in respect of a province in a fiscal year, as determined by the Minister under section 27, must be recovered out of any moneys payable to the province under this Act or as a debt due to the Government of Canada by the province.

If the Minister determines that he or she has over-recovered any amount recoverable from a province under this Part, he or she must, within the prescribed time and in the prescribed manner, pay that province an amount equal to the over-recovery.
If the Minister determines that he or she has under-recovered any amount recoverable from a province under this Part, he or she must recover an amount equal to the under-recovery either

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the prescribed time and in the prescribed manner, from any amount payable under this Act to that province, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from that province as a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada.

Section.Text: The amounts authorized under this Part to be paid by the Minister must be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund at such times and in such manner as may be prescribed.
Paragraph 40(a.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the information that must be prepared and submitted by the Chief Statistician of Canada for the purposes of Parts I, I.1 and V.1;

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the Canada Health Act

Section.Text: The definition cash contribution in section 2 of the Canada Health Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: cash contribution means the cash contribution in respect of the Canada Health Transfer that may be provided to a province under sections 24.2 and 24.21 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act;

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to this Act, as part of the Canada Health Transfer, a full cash contribution is payable by Canada to each province for each fiscal year.
Paragraph 13(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall give recognition to the Canada Health Transfer in any public documents, or in any advertising or promotional material, relating to insured health services and extended health care services in the province.

Section.Text: Paragraph 22(1)(d) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the manner in which recognition to the Canada Health Transfer is required to be given under paragraph 13(b).

Section.Text: The Fisheries Act is amended by adding the following after section 9:

Subsection.Text: For the proper management and control of fisheries and the conservation and protection of fish, the Minister may determine a quantity of fish or of fishing gear and equipment that may be allocated for the purpose of financing scientific and fisheries management activities that are described in a joint project agreement entered into with any person or body, or any federal or provincial minister, department or agency.
The Minister may specify, in a licence issued under this Act, a quantity of fish or of fishing gear and equipment allocated for the purpose of financing those activities.

Section.Text: Subsection 4(2) of the Food and Drugs Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A food does not have a poisonous or harmful substance in or on it for the purposes of paragraph (1)(a) — or is not adulterated for the purposes of paragraph (1)(d) — by reason only that it has in or on it a pest control product as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, or any of its components or derivatives, if the amount of the pest control product or the components or derivatives in or on the food being sold does not exceed the maximum residue limit specified under section 9 or 10 of that Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 29:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, the Minister may establish a list that sets out prescription drugs, classes of prescription drugs or both.
The list is not a regulation within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.
A regulation made under this Act may incorporate by reference the list established under subsection 29.1(1), either as it exists on a particular date or as it is amended from time to time.
The Minister shall ensure that the list that is incorporated by reference in the regulation is accessible.

A person is not liable to be found guilty of an offence for any contravention in respect of which the list that is incorporated by reference in the regulation is relevant unless, at the time of the alleged contravention, the list was accessible as required by subsection (2) or it was otherwise accessible to the person.
Subsection 30(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the establishment by the Minister of the list referred to in subsection 29.1(1), including amendments to it;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 30(1)(r) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: respecting marketing authorizations, including establishing the eligibility criteria for submitting an application for such authorizations or for amending such authorizations.

Section.Text: Section 30 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: A regulation made under this Act may establish classes and distinguish among those classes.

Section.Text: Section 30.2 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(r), the Minister may issue a marketing authorization that exempts — if the conditions, if any, to which the marketing authorization is subject are met — an advertisement, or a representation on a label, with respect to a food from the application, in whole or in part, of subsection 3(1) or (2) or any provision of the regulations specified in the marketing authorization.
The marketing authorization may be subject to any condition that the Minister considers appropriate.

Subject to regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(r), the Minister may issue a marketing authorization that exempts — if the conditions to which the marketing authorization is subject are met — a food from the application, in whole or in part, of paragraph 4(1)(a) or (d) or section 6 or 6.1 or any provision of the regulations specified in the marketing authorization.
The marketing authorization may be subject to any condition relating to the amount of any substance that may or must be in or on the food, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum residue limit of an agricultural chemical and its components or derivatives, singly or in any combination;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum residue limit of a veterinary drug and its metabolites, singly or in any combination;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum level of use for a food additive; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the minimum or maximum level, or both, of a vitamin, a mineral nutrient or an amino acid.

Subsection.Text: The marketing authorization may be subject to any other condition that the Minister considers appropriate.

Section.Text: A marketing authorization may establish classes and distinguish among those classes.
